notes: Pages with viewport-dominating consent UI on US IP. Screenshot source per page: 'v2' means re-use from research-v2/results/<run>/trajectory/, 'v3' means freshly captured into research-v3/screenshots/<id>/.

pages.notes: v1 documented this as the strongest US failure: vision model returned zero articles on the vanilla viewport. Hero candidate.
Bottom-anchored OneTrust banner, smaller viewport area than theverge. Control case -- banner doesn't dominate viewport.
Custom CCPA modal. v2 ghostery viewport is clean. Today the Guardian swaps in a donation modal after rejecting cookies -- behavior changed on the Guardian's side between 2026-05-18 and 2026-05-20.
Center-screen 'About Your Privacy' modal covers ~65% of viewport. Ghostery removes cleanly.
Bottom 'We Care About Your Privacy' modal with 340 partners. Ghostery removes cleanly.
Bottom 'Privacy Notice' modal. Ghostery removes cleanly.
Bottom 'We Care About Your Privacy' modal with 1008 partners. Ghostery removes cleanly.
Left-side 'Your Privacy Choices' panel. Ghostery removes cleanly.

candidates.notes: Vox Media stack; likely Sourcepoint TCF.
Newer outlet; sometimes shows a 'pay or accept' card.
Sports; sometimes interrupts with subscription overlays.
Has an explicit rule in autoconsent. UK-based but serves US visitors.
